title: Té Amo — Tea and Art House (research dump)
html:
TL;DR

  • Resource-list dump for Té Amo — Yahir's tea-and-art-house concept — pulled together late December 2023 alongside the operational pop up plan written one day later
  • Bookmarks LA-specific permitting paths: business.lacity.gov startup guide, restaurant/bar checklist, coffee shop California permits via Toast POS blog, art-gallery LLC how-tos
  • Sourcing leads: LA Herb and Wild Terra LA for herbs, Talavera Salazar for heart-shape ceramics ("corazones" line) — locks in the visual identity before the menu
  • Reference reading: tea-business primer at teaformeplease.com, gallery-commission economics, Peerspace as a host-the-space play, curate.la as the LA art-discovery layer
  • This doc is the due diligence half of the Té Amo cluster; the next day doc is the what we'd actually do half

Notes

A bare research-dump page from December 29, 2023, paired with the next-day's operational planning doc. No prose — just URLs in three buckets: (1) resources (LA permitting, coffee/tea business primers, gallery-economics writeups), (2) products (LA-area herb suppliers, heart-shape ceramics).

The doc is small but locks in a few things worth surfacing:

  • Geographic frame is LA-specific — every link points at LA city/county compliance, not Corvallis or Austin. Té Amo was conceived as a Los Angeles project from the start, predating the Austin thesis.
  • The visual identity precedes the menu — Yahir picked the Talavera Salazar heart-shape ceramics ("corazones" line) before nailing tea selection or pricing. The aesthetic was the anchor, the business mechanics second.
  • Gallery-commission research suggests a hybrid play — not just a tea shop with art on the walls, but a tea shop that takes commissions on art sold, which is meaningfully harder to operate.

This is the earliest Té Amo artifact in the vault and worth densifying when the project surfaces in 2024–2025 raws. Companion: 2023 12 30 current thoughts on te amo tea and art house.
